article_title: FDA Accepts Ultragenyx's Gene Therapy Application for Sanfilippo Syndrome, Setting September Decision Date for First-Ever Treatment

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: FDA Accepts Ultragenyx's Gene Therapy Application for Sanfilippo Syndrome, Setting September Decision Date for First-Ever Treatment
content_preview.summary: The FDA has accepted Ultragenyx's resubmitted BLA for UX111, a one-time gene therapy for the fatal childhood disease Sanfilippo syndrome Type A, with a decision expected by September 19, 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has accepted Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical's resubmitted Biologics License Application (BLA) for UX111, a one-time intravenous gene therapy designed to treat Sanfilippo syndrome Type A, according to [a company announcement on April 2, 2026](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/04/02/3267337/20739/en/Ultragenyx-Announces-U-S-FDA-Acceptance-of-BLA-Resubmission-for-UX111-AAV-Gene-Therapy-to-Treat-Sanfilippo-Syndrome-Type-A-MPS-IIIA.html). Th...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-sourced rare disease article with 3 GlobeNewsWire press releases providing primary documentation.
editor_notes.source_verification: GlobeNewsWire confirms FDA BLA acceptance, UX111 rebisufligene etisparvovec, September 19 PDUFA, Sanfilippo Type A, prior CRL manufacturing-only basis, up to 8 years follow-up, 23-point cognitive advantage. Human-requested with heightened scrutiny.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified via official Ultragenyx press releases.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Sanfilippo syndrome or Ultragenyx coverage.
